opp.name: ABM_Opp_Feb'26
opp.ais_why:
• On the Feb 2026 call, Maisam Raza confirmed ABM is locking in Coupa SIM in under 30 days and has 'no immediate opportunity' for any change. She liked Zycus's Teams integration and AI-driven automation conceptually but on her way out.
• She's planning to research Zycus on YouTube post go-live and stay in touch via LinkedIn for future roles. No mention of agentic, Merlin, or any AI POC interest at ABM.
• Opp is in Qualified with no Next Step recorded. Conversation is a courtesy networking touchpoint, not a buying motion. AI excitement belongs to the individual, not the account.

sources: Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam acknowledged Zycus ROI metrics but applied them to future learning, not an ABM business case
No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c) populated
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, ROI target, or savings metric was discussed or captured. The 03 Feb 2026 intro call with Maisam Raza was exploratory and educational; Maisam acknowledged Zycus's ROI claims (Delta's 3x to 12x ROI increase, 50% sourcing cycle time reduction, 70% reduced sourcing event hours) but stated these were learnings for a future role, not a current ABM business case. No pain quantification, no cost-savings target, no efficiency metric tied to ABM's operations. The deal was opened as a future-state placeholder with no active value case. To establish Metrics, the deal team must re-engage Maisam (or a new ABM stakeholder if she has moved on), confirm ABM's current procurement pain points post-Coupa implementation, and co-develop a quantified business case anchored to ABM's $1.1B spend base and 9,200 supplier footprint.

sources: Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam engaged and receptive but stated 'no opportunity for ABM right now' and 'I'll take the learnings with me to any new company'
No OpportunityContactRole records; Maisam Raza not formally tied to the opportunity
No re-engagement or forward motion since 03 Feb 2026 (143 days ago)
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Maisam Raza, the sole buyer participant across the two recorded calls (01 Aug 2025 initial quick connect with a different ABM contact, Daniel Ford, and 03 Feb 2026 intro call with Maisam), is a procurement technology user and influencer but explicitly stated she lacks decision authority ('I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks'). On the 03 Feb 2026 call, Maisam was receptive and intellectually engaged—she appreciated the Zycus Teams integration, AI negotiation agent, and ROI metrics, and said 'I think this would be beneficial' and offered to relay the conversation to her director in a meeting the following week. However, she framed the entire discussion as learning for a future role ('I'll take the learnings with me to any new company that I join'), not as advocacy for an ABM evaluation. She has not championed Zycus internally, introduced the deal team to the CPO or CTO, or driven any forward motion. The follow-up call scheduled for 07 Aug 2025 (likely a typo; should be 2025 based on call sequence, but if the 03 Feb call is 2026 then the follow-up would be later) was not recorded and no subsequent engagement occurred. Maisam's engagement is passive and cooling; she has not re-engaged in over 4 months (since 03 Feb 2026). To develop a champion, the deal team must re-engage Maisam, confirm whether she is still at ABM (LinkedIn check), assess her willingness to advocate, and multi-thread to identify a senior procurement or technology leader (CPO Joseph DeMarco, or a VP Operations/Procurement) who can sponsor an evaluation and influence the CTO.

sources: Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam confirmed Coupa go-live Nov 2024, Coupa SIM launching <30 days, CTO is 'Coupa fan'
Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam stated 'I have a laundry list of things I don't like in Coupa' but 'I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks'
Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: competitive landscape discussion included Ariba, SAP, GEP, Zip but was educational, not an active bake-off
competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent and the sole confirmed competitor. ABM implemented Coupa in November 2024 (fiscal year start) as part of a broader ERP rollout (JD Edwards), replacing Buysmart. On the 03 Feb 2026 intro call, Maisam Raza confirmed Coupa was live for P2P (accounts payable, PO management, invoicing, requisitioning) and that Coupa SIM (Supplier Information Management) was launching within 30 days. She described the CTO as a 'Coupa fan' who drove the platform selection, and noted that despite her personal frustrations with the relevant stakeholder and customer support ('99% not what they sold me' for Analytics and SIM, 'I can't just tolerate the UI in 2026'), the organization is committed to the relevant stakeholder procurement model is GPO-like (rebill with markup), $1.1B spend, 9,200 suppliers (reduced from 40,000 through consolidation), and Coupa is deeply embedded. No other competitors (Ariba, GEP, Zip, etc.) are actively in play; the 03 Feb call included a competitive landscape discussion where Maisam compared her experience with Ariba, SAP, Buysmart/GEP, and Salesforce, but this was educational context, not an active evaluation. Zycus's wedge is Coupa's limitations (UI, customer support, analytics/SIM under-delivery) and Zycus's Teams-based orchestration and AI negotiation agent, which Maisam found compelling. However, Coupa's incumbency is strong, the CTO is a Coupa advocate, and ABM is early in its Coupa journey (7-8 months post-go-live as of Feb 2026), making a near-term displacement unlikely. The path forward is to monitor for a Coupa renewal window (likely 3-year contract from ~2024, so renewal discussion would be ~2026-2027), position Zycus as the alternative when ABM reassesses, and multi-thread to counter the CTO's Coupa bias.

sources: Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam stated 'I have a laundry list of things that I don't like in Coupa... I barely make my ends meet with Coupa'
Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam noted Coupa Analytics and SIM were '99% not what they sold me'
Avoma intro call 03 Feb 2026: Maisam praised Buysmart/GEP customer success over Coupa's 'raise a ticket' model
identify_pain.narrative: Maisam Raza articulated specific operational pain with Coupa on the 03 Feb 2026 intro call, but the pain was framed as a personal frustration rather than an organizational mandate to change. She stated: 'I have a laundry list of things that I don't like in Coupa, but I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks. I barely make my ends meet with the relevant stakeholder highlighted Coupa's UI as a major limitation ('I can't just tolerate it in 2026') and noted that Coupa Analytics and SIM were '99% not what they sold me.' She contrasted this unfavorably with her prior experience with Buysmart/GEP, where customer success and responsiveness were superior; with Coupa, the answer to any issue is 'this is a Coupa limitation' or 'raise a ticket and it will be delivered in a year or two years.' However, Maisam also made clear that ABM had just gone live with Coupa (Nov 2024) and was launching Coupa SIM within 30 days, so the organization was committed to the platform despite her personal reservations. The pain owner is Maisam (a user/influencer), not the CTO (the decision-maker who is a 'Coupa fan'). No organizational pain tied to business impact (e.g., cost, cycle time, compliance risk) was quantified. To convert this to a live opportunity, the deal team must re-engage, validate whether Maisam's pain is shared by other ABM stakeholders (CPO, procurement ops leads), quantify the operational/financial impact, and identify a triggering event (e.g., Coupa renewal, a major Coupa limitation blocking a business initiative) that would open a re-evaluation window.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process, contracting mechanics, or legal/procurement workflow discussed. The 03 Feb 2026 intro call was purely exploratory and product-education focused; no MSA, SOW, InfoSec review, or contract timeline was mentioned. Maisam Raza indicated ABM operates a GPO-like rebill model with tight margins (6% net on $9B revenue) and had recently implemented Coupa and JD Edwards ERP, but no contracting process, partner involvement (SI/implementation partner), or procurement approval workflow was mapped. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c or Mandate__c fields populated. To establish Paper Process, the deal team must re-engage, confirm Coupa contract term and renewal timeline, understand ABM's legal/InfoSec review requirements for SaaS procurement platforms, and map any SI or implementation partner relationships (e.g., did ABM use a consulting firm for the Coupa rollout?).

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Maisam Raza, the sole participant in the 03 Feb 2026 intro call, described herself as knowledgeable about procurement technology but not the budget authority; she deferred commercial and strategic platform decisions to her CTO (who she described as a 'Coupa fan' whose opinion she could not overrule). No contact with the relevant stakeholder (Joseph DeMarco, known from account-level contacts), CFO, or any C-level sponsor. Zero OpportunityContactRole records. The buyer made it clear ABM had recently implemented Coupa and ERP (go-live Nov 2024 for Coupa, Coupa SIM launching within 30 days of the Feb call) and was not in a buying window. No budget discussion, no approval-chain mapping, no commercial conversation. To map the EB, the deal team must multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder (CPO), confirm who controls procurement platform investment decisions, and secure an executive conversation to validate budget authority and appetite for a future evaluation.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. The 03 Feb 2026 intro call was a single exploratory conversation with Maisam Raza, a procurement technology user/influencer who explicitly stated ABM had just implemented Coupa (Nov 2024) and ERP and was not in a buying cycle ('no opportunity for ABM right now'). She indicated the CTO was the Coupa advocate and decision-maker for procurement platform strategy, but no process for revisiting that decision was discussed. No mention of approval chain, vendor evaluation process, budget cycle, or contract renewal timeline. The What_is_the_decision_process__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c fields are not populated. The call ended with Maisam planning to relay Zycus learnings to her director in a meeting the following week, with a follow-up call scheduled for 07 Aug 2025 (Thursday), but that follow-up was not recorded (marked as not_recorded gap in Avoma manifest) and no subsequent buyer engagement occurred. To establish Decision Process, the deal team must re-engage, confirm whether ABM is approaching a Coupa renewal or re-evaluation window, map the approval chain (CTO, CPO, CFO), and identify what would trigger a platform comparison.

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria established. The 03 Feb 2026 intro call was a Zycus product overview and competitive positioning discussion, not a requirements or evaluation session. Maisam Raza expressed interest in Zycus's Teams-based orchestration layer, AI-driven negotiation agent, and user experience (contrasting it favorably with the relevant stakeholder limitations), but she did not articulate ABM's evaluation criteria, RFP/RFI requirements, or success metrics for a future platform decision. No Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, or AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields populated. The conversation was educational and forward-looking ('I'll take the learnings with me to any new company'), not a live evaluation. To establish Decision Criteria, the deal team must re-engage, confirm ABM's current pain points with Coupa (Maisam listed 'laundry list of limitations' but did not detail them), map what would trigger a re-evaluation, and co-develop afit assessment against ABM's procurement model (GPO-like rebill structure, 9,200 suppliers, $1.1B spend, materials consolidation focus).

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS (AI Score) fields populated in Salesforce. Based on the Avoma calls, ABM shows moderate AI curiosity but not active AI hunger. Maisam Raza was intrigued by Zycus's AI negotiation agent and acknowledged the ROI potential, but ABM's recent Coupa implementation (Nov 2024) and focus on data cleanup and SIM go-live suggest the organization is in stabilization mode, not innovation/AI acceleration mode. Maisam noted ABM operates a GPO-like rebill model with tight margins (6% net on $9B revenue), implying cost discipline and efficiency focus, which could make AI-driven ROI compelling in a future buying window. However, the CTO's Coupa advocacy and lack of current pain or urgency signal AI Curious, not AI Hungry.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Maisam Raza is a receptive influencer but lacks decision authority, has not advocated internally, and has not re-engaged in 143 days. She framed the 03 Feb 2026 conversation as learning for a future role, not as an ABM opportunity. Daniel Ford (Aug 2025 contact) was similarly positive but passive, with no follow-through after the 07 Aug 2025 call. Neither has introduced Zycus to decision-makers (CPO, CTO) or driven any forward motion. Champion development is the single highest-priority action to resurrect this deal.

Quantify savings potential (50% sourcing cycle time reduction applied to ABM's 9,200 supplier base, AI-driven negotiation applied to $1.1B spend) and contrast with Coupa's Analytics/SIM shortfalls.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal created after explicit 'no opportunity now' statement; 143 days without buyer engagement since sole intro call. Coupa deeply entrenched with CTO sponsorship; contact lacks authority and has not re-engaged despite product interest.

flags: Single-threaded and no champion: Only Maisam Raza engaged (user/influencer, no decision authority, passive). Zero OpportunityContactRole records. No contact with the relevant stakeholder, CTO (Coupa advocate), or any procurement leadership. Multi-thread immediately or disqualify.

Misaligned close date: Close date 30 Jun 2027 (369 days out) implies a long sales cycle, but the deal is in early Qualified stage with no discovery depth, no demo, no validation milestone, and no forward motion in 143 days. This is not a 2027 pipeline deal; it is a speculative future conversation that never converted to an active opportunity.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a dormant future-state conversation, not a live opportunity. The buyer explicitly stated no near-term need, the CTO is a Coupa advocate, and no engagement has occurred in 143 days. The deal should be disqualified or moved to a long-term nurture track, not actively forecasted. Critical gaps: no champion, no EB, no multi-threading, no buying window, no business case.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole active competitor and a deeply entrenched incumbent. ABM implemented Coupa in November 2024 (7-8 months before the Feb 2026 intro call) as part of an ERP rollout, and was launching Coupa SIM within 30 days of that call. The CTO is a Coupa advocate ('Coupa fan') and drove the platform selection. Maisam Raza, a procurement technology user, articulated significant dissatisfaction with Coupa's UI, customer support, and under-delivery on Analytics and SIM promises ('99% not what they sold me'), and praised Zycus's Teams integration and AI negotiation agent as differentiated and compelling. However, her frustration is personal and not organizational; she lacks decision authority and stated the organization is committed to Coupa despite her reservations. No other competitors are in play. The threat level is HIGH due to incumbency, recent implementation, and CTO sponsorship, but Coupa is vulnerable on user experience, customer support, and unmet Analytics/SIM expectations. Zycus's path to displacement is long-term: monitor for Coupa renewal (likely 2026-2027 discussion window if 3-year contract), position against Coupa's weaknesses, and multi-thread to procurement and technology leaders who can counter the CTO's bias. As of Jun 2026, the deal is dormant and Coupa faces no active challenge.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I have a laundry list of things that I don't like in Coupa, but I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks. I barely make my ends meet with Coupa. 99% not what they sold me. I can't just tolerate in 2026. (Maisam Raza, 03 Feb 2026)
competitors.sentiment: negative from user (Maisam Raza), positive from decision-maker (CTO)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus's Teams-based orchestration layer (eliminates Coupa's UI friction) and AI negotiation agent (addresses Coupa's Analytics/SIM under-delivery) as the user-centric, innovation-led alternative. Target Coupa renewal window (~2026-2027) and multi-thread to CPO and procurement ops leaders to build a coalition that can challenge the CTO's Coupa preference. Anchor the business case to user productivity, faster sourcing cycles (50% reduction), and ROI (Delta 3x-12x case study).

ai_positioning_strength.score: Moderate (well-articulated to influencer; not yet positioned to decision-maker)
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI capabilities (Merlin AI negotiation agent, Teams-based orchestration, autonomous sourcing) were well-received by Maisam Raza on the 03 Feb 2026 intro call. She responded positively to the Delta ROI case study (3x-12x improvement, 50% sourcing cycle time reduction, 70% reduced sourcing event hours) and stated 'This is really cool. I haven't seen that in Coupa.' However, the AI positioning was educational and aspirational, not tied to an active ABM business case or buying initiative. Maisam's interest was personal ('I'll take the learnings with me to any new company') rather than organizational. The CTO (Coupa advocate) has not been exposed to the AI narrative. The deal is not under-positioned on AI capability, but the positioning has not reached the decision-maker or converted to a live evaluation.

items.quote: This Teams integration, which we've been doing for a number of years now... if they can use Teams, they can literally interact with our tool. (Richard Hunsinger, 03 Feb 2026). Maisam responded: 'This is really cool. I haven't seen that in Coupa. I didn't see that in Salesforce.' (03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: Teams-based intake and orchestration layer (avoid complex UI training)

items.quote: We've developed an autonomous negotiation agent... it's getting to be a pretty evolved technology. (Richard Hunsinger, 03 Feb 2026). Maisam noted Delta's ROI increase from 3x to 12x and sourcing cycle time reduction of 50% as compelling. (03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: AI-driven sourcing and negotiation automation (reduce manual spreadsheet work, improve ROI)

items.quote: The reason people select us is this Teams integration... our tool is also very easy to use because back in 2018 we created this thing we called 'do the drops', recognized as a really great user interface. (Richard Hunsinger, 03 Feb 2026). Maisam contrasted this with Coupa: 'I can't just tolerate in 2026.' (03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: Superior UI/UX and customer support (pain point with Coupa)

items.quote: We're an open API platform. We've developed this whole extensive platform called AppExtend... it takes development of something new from maybe months and years to weeks. (Richard Hunsinger, 03 Feb 2026). Maisam asked about integrations to Relish, Avetta, Supplier.io, TIN validation, and diversity certification agencies; Richard confirmed Zycus supports these. (03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: Rapid third-party integrations (Avetta, Supplier.io, risk/compliance tools, TIN validation)

items.quote: This is not an opportunity for ABM right now because big companies don't change ERPs very often or P2P technologies very often, but I'll take the learnings with me to any new company that I join. (Maisam Raza, 03 Feb 2026)
I have a laundry list of things that I don't like in Coupa, but I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks. So I barely make my ends meet with Coupa. (Maisam Raza, 03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: Executive sponsorship and decision authority (CTO is Coupa advocate)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: ABM's stated needs—Teams-based intake orchestration, AI-driven sourcing automation, improved UI/UX over Coupa, superior customer support, and integration with JD Edwards ERP—align well with Zycus's platform strengths (Teams integration, Merlin AI negotiation agent, AppExtend for rapid integrations, customer-centric development). However, the timing and organizational readiness are misaligned: ABM just implemented Coupa (Nov 2024) and is not in a buying cycle. The CTO is a Coupa advocate, creating a sponsorship gap. Fit is strong on capability, weak on timing and internal sponsorship.

hard.ais_why:
• On the Feb 2026 call, Maisam Raza confirmed ABM is locking in Coupa SIM in under 30 days and has 'no immediate opportunity' for any change. She liked Zycus's Teams integration and AI-driven automation conceptually but on her way out.
• She's planning to research Zycus on YouTube post go-live and stay in touch via LinkedIn for future roles. No mention of agentic, Merlin, or any AI POC interest at ABM.
• Opp is in Qualified with no Next Step recorded. Conversation is a courtesy networking touchpoint, not a buying motion. AI excitement belongs to the individual, not the account.

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 7 day(s) ago (2026-06-19).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Ghost deal: No buyer engagement in 143 days (since 03 Feb 2026 intro call). Maisam Raza stated 'no opportunity for ABM right now' and has not re-engaged. Deal is cold and should be disqualified or moved to long-term nurture.

Coupa incumbency unchallenged: CTO is 'Coupa fan' who drove platform selection. ABM implemented Coupa Nov 2024 (19 months ago as of Jun 2026) and is committed despite Maisam's personal frustrations. No active displacement effort; Coupa renewal window (~2026-2027) is the only viable entry point. Position for long-term re-evaluation, not near-term close.

No business case or value quantification: Maisam acknowledged Zycus's Delta ROI case study but no ABM-specific value case, savings target, or efficiency metric developed. Without a quantified business case tied to ABM's $1.1B spend and Coupa pain points, there is no economic justification to displace an incumbent 19 months post-implementation.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: I have a laundry list of things that I don't like in Coupa, but I cannot overrule what the CTO thinks. I barely make my ends meet with Coupa. 99% not what they sold me. I can't just tolerate in 2026. (Maisam Raza, 03 Feb 2026)
value.sentiment: negative from user (Maisam Raza), positive from decision-maker (CTO)
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus's Teams-based orchestration layer (eliminates Coupa's UI friction) and AI negotiation agent (addresses Coupa's Analytics/SIM under-delivery) as the user-centric, innovation-led alternative. Target Coupa renewal window (~2026-2027) and multi-thread to CPO and procurement ops leaders to build a coalition that can challenge the CTO's Coupa preference. Anchor the business case to user productivity, faster sourcing cycles (50% reduction), and ROI (Delta 3x-12x case study).
